content.full_text:
December 11, 1990

Pacific Telesis Group -
Legislation - Political Reform Act of 1974

VIA FEDERAL EXPRESS

Ms. Carla Wardlow
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Carla:

Pursuant to our recent conversations, this letter is a request for formal written advice pursuant to Government Code section 83114(b) on behalf of our client, the Pacific Telesis Group.

Employees of Pacific Telesis Group subsidiaries from time to time appear as parties before the California Public Utilities Commission (the "PUC") in various proceedings, as well as meetings, conferences or workshops ancillary to those proceedings. Attendance at the meetings would ordinarily not qualify as "administrative testimony" under recent FPPC advice letters. Selby Advice Letter A-88-387; see also Gardner Advice Letter A-90-307 and Lowell Advice Letter I-90-574. However, in some cases no "qualifying official," as defined in 2 Cal.Code Regs. § 18239(d)(5), is present at these meetings.

We note that a person cannot become a lobbyist unless he or she, for compensation, engages in direct communication, other than administrative testimony, with a qualifying official for the purpose of influencing legislative or administrative action. 2 Cal.Code Regs. § 18239. Please confirm that a person who never engages in any direct

Ms. Carla Wardlow  
December 11, 1990  
Page 2

communication with a qualifying official can never qualify as a lobbyist.

We appreciate your attention on this matter. If you have any questions, please contact me.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Frederick K. Lowell

cc: Kristina Veaco, Esq.
